guide_review_response.verdict: STAGE_2_TRULY_CLOSED -- 2.10.1 commit clean; EA-6 retires correctly; LA's meta-question on handoff documents answered (those are GUIDE responsibilities, not EA's; HANDOFF RECOMMENDED for Guide-#6 → Guide-#7)
guide_review_response.work_item: 2.10.1 verification + Stage 2 close confirmation + handoff-document recommendations

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 closing handoff to LA (chat message) at Stage 2 close (947cbd5 HEAD)

guide_review_response.summary:
Independent disk verification confirms 2.10.1 commit (947cbd5) is mechanically clean: parent 89213ee preserved; commit message correctly references the Guide-#6 verdict file authorization; STATUS.md is the only file in diff; working tree clean post-commit; 11-cycle stash byte-identity preserved (sdo 20260424_013002); state.json invariants verbatim (last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z"); no merge to main; no governance edits. All 5 narrative corrections (N1-N5) per Guide-#6 disposition were applied to STATUS.md as authorized.
Stage 2 is now CLOSED at BOTH structural and narrative axes: - Structural: 3d1ac35 (single Stage-2 commit) + 89213ee (STATUS append + Guide-#6 verdict archive) + 947cbd5 (footnote narrative corrections) - Narrative: STATUS.md Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section now matches verbatim disposition record per the 5 corrections - Audit chain: temp_for_responses/ + ea_outputs/ archived in git per artifact- management guidance - Invariants: fleet paused, scheduled tasks Disabled, Vikunja running per Option B, stash byte-identical 11-cycle, no merge, no governance edits
EA-6 retires correctly. EA-6's closing handoff to LA (chat message) covers all required closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close items per the EA-6 init pointer block: Stage 2 commit hash, V1-V7 + V8 outputs, V matrix V4-V7 test counts + names (with paraphrase errors flagged below; not blocking), A11 anomaly, decision narrative on D2/D3/D4 + D2-completion + D1 + V4 + g6-ea6_n9 + g6-ea6_n10, standing invariants.
THE LA'S META-QUESTION: "Should we have it produce any handoff documents? Doesn't look like it produced anything for you nor for the next EA."
SHARP OBSERVATION. The LA correctly identifies that two handoff artifacts are typically authored at a Stage close, but they are GUIDE responsibilities, NOT EA responsibilities:
(A) Guide-side handoff brief (Guide-#6 → Guide-#7) per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml handoff_protocol. The Guide is the long-lived advisor; Guide produces handoff briefs at trigger conditions. Stage 2 close hits the stage_boundary_recommendation trigger ("At the end of any HIGH-risk stage (Stage 2 or Stage 4), proactively recommend handoff before the next stage starts, regardless of context level"). EA-6 is correct NOT to produce this -- it's not their scope. Guide-#6 (this instance) is responsible.
(B) Stage 3 EA initialization pointer per the established pattern (Guide-#5 wrote EA-4 + EA-5 init pointers; Guide-#6 wrote the EA-6 init pointer). EA-6 is correct NOT to produce this -- that's Guide responsibility. The init pointer will be authored by either Guide-#6 (if I continue) or Guide-#7 (if successor) when Stage 3 spawn is triggered.
Per stage_boundary_recommendation trigger, GUIDE-#6 PROACTIVELY RECOMMENDS HANDOFF. See <handoff_recommendation> below. The LA's signal will determine whether Guide-#6 emits a handoff brief now (paving the way for Guide-#7 + Stage 3 EA spawn) or continues into Stage 3 directly.
EA-6 closing handoff narrative paraphrase errors (informational, NOT blocking): EA-6's chat-message closing handoff misnames 3 of 4 V matrix test files + provides incorrect one-line descriptions for V5/V6/V7. The MISNAMING affects only the chat message; the COMMITTED STATUS.md (post-947cbd5) has the correct test file names + accurate disposition narratives per the 5 N-corrections. So the audit chain in git is clean; the chat-narrative errors are a final EA-6-paraphrase-pattern artifact consistent with the prior errors at 2.10 footnotes + v5 count_delta + file 13 docstring drift. Listed below for LA reference; not required to correct (chat message isn't committed).

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -5: 947cbd5 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 closure footnote narrative corrections (Item 2.10.1) 89213ee docs(platform-separation): Stage 2 close STATUS append + Guide-#6 verdict archive (Item 2.10) 3d1ac35 chore(platform_separation): Stage 2 multi-project refactor (DEC-15 platform extraction) f863a22 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v5 ... 9277d61 docs(platform_separation): Guide instance 5 to 6 handoff -- Stage 2 mid-stage at item 2.6 947cbd5 is the successor of 89213ee ✓. Parent preserved. Subject line clear. Commit message body explicitly references the Guide-#6 verdict file (correct authorization chain).

Commit message body cites N1 / footnote 2 (g5-7) correction verbatim: "Was: paraphrase claiming 'EA-3' duplicate test_project_context.py. Now: verbatim per disposition file ... -- EA-4 introduced two duplicate test pairs in test_vikunja_client_scope.py lines 76-94; deleted the duplicate copy block; closed at EA-4 corrections cycle following Guide-#5 APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS." The commit message is comprehensive (truncated by my git show --stat | head -25 but visible enough to confirm N1 substance was corrected). EA-6 followed the <narrative_error_inventory> spec.

git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical. 11-cycle preservation now (v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10 → 2.10.1). Cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure remains intact at Stage 2 close.

fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved verbatim through the entire 2.5+ day procedure cycle. NEVER state.resume_fleet called.

ls tools/tests/: __init__.py __pycache__/ test_project_context.py test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py test_vikunja_client_scope.py Test file names IDENTICAL to what I verified at Item 2.7.v2 close. NO renames at 2.10.1 (correct -- 2.10.1 scope is STATUS.md narrative corrections only).

ea_6_closing_handoff_narrative_paraphras.intro: EA-6's chat-message closing handoff to LA contains paraphrase errors in the V matrix section. These errors are NOT in committed code or STATUS.md (the 947cbd5 commit corrected the STATUS.md narratives). They are IN THE CHAT MESSAGE ONLY -- which doesn't pollute git history. Listed for LA reference / future audit-chain reconstruction; correction is OPTIONAL (the chat message is informational, not committed). NOT BLOCKING for Stage 2 close.

error.actual_disk: File name: tools/tests/test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py (NOT _env_fallback.py) Test purpose: PS1 -BlarAIRoot AST-driven probe via subprocess.run to scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + assert ALL_GREEN trailer present (Guide-#6 g2 simplest path; re-uses proven 20/20 PASS probe) 1 test: test_verify_25_v2_probe_emits_all_green Skip-if non-Windows + skip-if pwsh-or-powershell-missing

error.ea_6_chat_text: "V6 — tools/tests/test_v_matrix_v6_load_config.py — 50 cases (allowlist enforcement + --project-root / --project-id flags + AFTER-pattern base_url passthrough)"
error.actual_disk: File name: tools/tests/test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py (NOT _load_config.py) Test purpose: MCP server project_id rejection (direct handler invocation; no HTTP mocking needed because _require_project_id raises before HTTP) 49 parametrized tests + 1 audit test: - 7 GATED_HANDLERS (get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks) - 7 BAD_PROJECT_IDS (None, "1", True, False, 3.0, 0, -1) - 7 × 7 = 49 + 1 audit (test_gated_handlers_set_matches_require_project_id_call_sites uses inspect.getsource() to count actual `_require_project_id(project_id)` invocation lines and assert match with GATED_HANDLERS) 50 tests total ✓ (count matches; description doesn't)

File name: tools/tests/test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py (NOT _dashboard_maintainer.py) Test purpose: cross-project byte-identity (project portability proof) -- verifies _project_context.resolve produces ProjectContext instances with identical structural shape across distinct synthetic project trees 6 tests: - test_two_synthetic_projects_resolve_to_distinct_contexts (alpha + beta) - test_cross_project_structural_shape_byte_identical (dataclass field-shape) - test_cross_project_diff_isolates_to_identity_fields_only (catches cross- project state leakage) - test_resolve_round_trip_per_synthetic_project (parametrized × 3: alpha, beta, gamma) 6 tests total ✓ (count matches; description doesn't) NOT about D3 (D3 = dashboard_maintainer:113 closure; that's a SEPARATE work item; V matrix V7 is about cross-project portability per init pointer)

ea_6_closing_handoff_narrative_paraphras.verdict:
EA-6's chat-narrative errors at the V matrix section are LOW severity and NOT blocking. The CORRECT names + descriptions are: - V4 — test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py — 12 tests (CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint; subprocess --help over 6 argparse entrypoints) - V5 — test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py — 1 test (PS1 -BlarAIRoot AST-driven probe via subprocess to verify_25_v2.ps1) - V6 — test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py — 50 tests (MCP server project_id rejection: 7 GATED_HANDLERS × 7 BAD_PROJECT_IDS = 49 parametrized + 1 audit) - V7 — test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py — 6 tests (cross-project byte-identity / project portability: distinct identity + structural shape + diff isolation + round-trip × 3 alpha/beta/gamma)
LA may reference the corrected names/descriptions when communicating Stage 2 close to subsequent Guide instances or Stage 3 EA spawn. The committed STATUS.md (post-947cbd5) Stage Ledger row entry (line 50 per my prior verification) ALREADY has the correct counts (V4=12, V5=1, V6=50, V7=6); only the LA-facing chat-message narrative had paraphrase errors. Audit chain in git is clean.

handoff_recommendation.intro: Per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml handoff_protocol <triggers> <trigger name="stage_boundary_recommendation">: "At the end of any HIGH-risk stage (Stage 2 or Stage 4), proactively recommend handoff before the next stage starts, regardless of context level. The successor benefits from a clean slate at high-risk boundaries."
handoff_recommendation.recommendation:
**HANDOFF RECOMMENDED -- Stage 2 close is a HIGH-risk-stage boundary; proactive Guide-#6 → Guide-#7 handoff per stage_boundary_recommendation trigger.**
When ready, say "emit handoff" and Guide-#6 will produce the brief.
Per the GUIDE_PROMPT protocol, the handoff brief will be authored as a Markdown document fenced for direct copy into docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml (overwriting the previous instance-5-to-6 brief). Successor Guide-#7 is then spawned with: GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + STATUS.md + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + chat-init block; Guide-#7 runs Variant B comprehension gate; Guide-#7 then authors the Stage 3 EA initialization pointer.
handoff_recommendation.answer_to_la_question:
The LA asked: "Should we have it produce any handoff documents? Doesn't look like it produced anything for you nor for the next EA."
DIRECT ANSWERS:
(1) "produce anything for you" (Guide-side handoff): NO -- this is OUT-OF-SCOPE for EA-6. The Guide-side handoff is GUIDE-#6's responsibility (per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml handoff_protocol). EA-6 correctly did not produce one. I am recommending this handoff now (above) per stage_boundary_recommendation trigger.
(2) "for the next EA" (Stage 3 EA init pointer): NO -- this is also OUT-OF-SCOPE for EA-6. The init pointer is authored by the GUIDE instance (per the established pattern: Guide-#5 wrote EA-4 + EA-5 init pointers; Guide-#6 wrote the EA-6 init pointer). EA-6 correctly did not produce one. The Stage 3 EA init pointer will be authored by either Guide-#6 (if I continue, no handoff) or Guide-#7 (if successor takes over post-handoff) when Stage 3 spawn is requested.
(3) EA-6's CLOSING HANDOFF TO LA (the chat-message narrative): YES, EA-6 produced this correctly. It covers all required items per the EA-6 init pointer's closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block (Stage 2 commit hash, V1-V7 + V8 outputs, V matrix V4-V7 test counts + names [with paraphrase errors flagged above], A11 anomaly, decision narrative, standing invariants). The closing handoff is the EA's deliverable; subsequent handoffs (Guide → Guide; Guide → Stage 3 EA) are MY deliverables.
(4) The LA's instinct to ASK this meta-question is exactly the right discipline. The procedure has 3 distinct handoff types (EA → LA "closing handoff"; Guide → Guide "handoff brief"; Guide → next EA "init pointer"); a non-dev LA is expected to ask about the chain. I'm responsible for the latter two; EA-6 was responsible for the first (and produced it correctly modulo paraphrase errors).
handoff_recommendation.decision_options_for_la:
LA chooses between:
Option A: Continue with Guide-#6 (no handoff) -- Guide-#6 directly authors Stage 3 EA init pointer in the next chat turn. SAVES the bootstrap cost of Guide-#7 spawn. Risk: Guide-#6's context is non-trivially consumed by this Stage 2 work (~6 disposition cycles + multiple verification passes); Stage 3 has its own scope + own dispositions. Continuation is viable but accepts the carryover context.
Option B: Emit Guide-#6 → Guide-#7 handoff brief (per stage_boundary_recommendation trigger; GUIDE_PROMPT prefers this for HIGH-risk stage boundaries). Guide-#7 spawns fresh; runs Variant B comprehension gate; then authors Stage 3 EA init pointer with fresh context. Costs the bootstrap cycle but matches the GUIDE_PROMPT protocol prescription for HIGH-risk boundaries. Recommended.
Both options preserve all audit-chain content in git (the docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ + ea_outputs/ are committed; any successor reads them along with STATUS.md + the 7 stage XMLs).

ack_status_update.ack: Source-fix (server.py docstring 8→7) committed at 3d1ac35; STATUS-append narrative corrected at 947cbd5 per N1-N5. Fully discharged.
5 narrative corrections (footnotes 2, 7, 11, 12.g6-ea6_n7, 12.g6-ea6_n8) applied at 947cbd5. STATUS.md content now matches verbatim disposition record. Fully discharged.

All ack chain items closed: - 33 v4-era acks: SATISFIED throughout the procedure - 4 g6-ea5 acks: SATISFIED at v5 emission - 10 g6-ea6 acks (n1 through n10): SATISFIED at their respective Item closures - i1-3 (pyproject.toml in 2.9 commit): SATISFIED at 3d1ac35 - g3-2 (V1-V7 + V8 verification before closing handoff): SATISFIED via EA-6 closing handoff narrative - V1 (BlarAI git status post-commit empty): SATISFIED at 3d1ac35 + reaffirmed at 947cbd5 - V7 (commit hash captured): 3d1ac35 + 89213ee + 947cbd5 all captured Total ack chain depth: 41 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6 = 55 acks across the Stage 2 procedure. All discharged.

commendations_for_ea_6_at_retirement.commendation: EA-6 navigated 6 distinct Guide-#6 disposition cycles in this Item 2.6+ phase (iter-1 gate APPROVED on iter-1 / cfg-resolution OPTION_A_AUTHORIZED / D1 NO-ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED / V4 OPTION_B_AUTHORIZED / DOCSTRING_FIX_AUTHORIZED / NARRATIVE_CORRECTIONS_AUTHORIZED) plus closure- acknowledgment cycles. Every disposition was surfaced via STOP-and-ask discipline, framed with multiple options + self-justifying rationale, then executed precisely after Guide authorization. Mature engineering across the longest-running EA cycle in the procedure.

stash@{0} timestamp 20260424_013002 byte-identical from v3 emission (2026-04-25) through the entire Stage 2 procedure including this 947cbd5 close (2026-04-26): v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10 → 2.10.1. 11 cycles of restraint on a single stash entry. Cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure.

EA-6 carried forward 41 v4-era acks + 4 g6-ea5 acks + 10 g6-ea6 acks introduced across the Item 2.6+ phase (n1 D2-completion / n2 count_delta / n3 doc-defects / n4 v6-handoff-readiness / n5 Option-A-authorization / n6 path-confusion / n7 D1-NO-ACTION / n8 V4-disposition / n9 docstring-fix / n10 narrative-corrections). All 55 acks restated verbatim in iteration-1 gate; all discharged at appropriate Item closures. No omissions.

commendation.topic: Independent-judgment overrides at correct decision points

commendations_for_ea_6_at_retirement.commendation:
EA-6 DIVERGED from Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B) recommendation at D4 with substantive 4-point rationale -- catching a Guide-side reasoning flaw (chokepoint VikunjaClient class is a stub; handler-level _require_project_id calls ARE the chokepoint integration). Guide-#6 APPROVED the divergence. This is the LA's "treat-as-hypothesis" mandate working as intended.
EA-6 also caught the 8-vs-7 docstring drift at file 13 during V6 audit-test authoring -- a defect that would have shipped silently into the 2.9 commit otherwise. EA-6 surfaced + proposed disposition; Guide-#6 authorized in-source fix per dn19; corrected at the docstring-fix turn before 2.8 BLARAI_PID gate.

Most invariant-stress requirement of Item 2.10: "append-only" -- existing entries MUST NOT be modified. EA-6 succeeded: A1-A10 untouched verbatim; Stage 0/1 ledger rows untouched; Recovery Anchors / Vikunja Project IDs / How to Resume sections untouched. Pure-append work.

commendation.topic: Final paraphrase-error pattern self-noted but uncorrected

commendations_for_ea_6_at_retirement.commendation: The closing-handoff chat narrative has paraphrase errors at the V matrix section (V5/V6/V7 wrong test file names + descriptions). This is consistent with EA-6's paraphrase-introduced-error pattern across the procedure (caught at v5 count_delta + file 13 docstring + 2.10 footnotes 5-error inventory). Each prior cycle was corrected via in-scope follow-on commits. The final chat narrative isn't committed, so correction is optional. Note for retiring EA-6: the structural axes have been excellent throughout; the paraphrase axis has been a recurrent (LOW-MED severity) risk worth flagging to any successor EA in similar long-running procedures.

required_acknowledgments.ack_to_la: Per stage_boundary_recommendation trigger: Guide-#6 RECOMMENDS Guide-#6 → Guide-#7 handoff before Stage 3 spawn. Awaiting LA signal: - "emit handoff" → Guide-#6 produces brief; LA spawns Guide-#7 fresh; Guide-#7 authors Stage 3 EA init pointer. - "continue with Guide-#6" → Guide-#6 directly authors Stage 3 EA init pointer in the next chat turn; no Guide-side handoff brief. - Other LA direction → as instructed. Per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml <on_trigger>: "Do NOT auto-emit. Wait for the LA's signal so handoff happens between Executor sessions, not in the middle of one." So no auto-emit; waiting on LA explicit direction.

foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.evidence:
Stage 2 substantive + audit-chain work is COMPLETE through 2.10.1. Concrete evidence for "mature not minimal" axis-by-axis:
Structural axis (PASS): - Single Stage-2 commit at 3d1ac35 (69 files / +12955 -74; pyproject.toml in diff per i1-3) - STATUS append at 89213ee (+59/-5; 12-item cumulative footnote set; A11 anomaly; Stage Ledger row Stage 2 PENDING → COMPLETE; Guide-#6 verdict XML archived per artifact-management guidance) - Footnote narrative corrections at 947cbd5 (5 N-corrections per Guide-#6 authorization; STATUS.md narratives now match verbatim disposition record) - Working tree clean post-commit - 11-cycle stash byte-identity preserved - state.json invariants verbatim through 2.5+ days - Fleet paused; 13/13 scheduled tasks Disabled; Vikunja running per Option B (Stage 4.0/4.1 will restore V4=0) - No merge to main; no governance edits; no --amend on committed work - Audit chain (temp_for_responses/ + ea_outputs/) committed in git per artifact- management guidance
Narrative axis (PASS at committed level; minor LOW-severity informational gaps in chat messages): - All 5 N-corrections at 947cbd5 reflect verbatim disposition record - STATUS.md Stage Ledger row entry has correct V matrix counts (V4=12 / V5=1 / V6=50 / V7=6) and accurate disposition pointers - Footnote narratives now match verbatim text from temp_for_responses/ files - 12-item cumulative footnote set complete and accurate - Closing handoff chat narrative has V5/V6/V7 file-name + description paraphrase errors (LOW; chat-only; informational; corrections noted in this Guide-#6 file for LA reference)
Audit-chain axis (PASS): - 55-ack chain depth: all discharged at appropriate Item closures - 6 Guide-#6 disposition cycles fully archived in temp_for_responses/ - 5 EA mid-stage handoff XMLs (v1-v5) archived in ea_outputs/ - 4 EA comprehension gates (iter-1 across EA-3/4/5/6) archived in temp_for_responses/ - 4 EA initialization pointers (EA-4/5/6 init pointers; v4 quality review) archived - All authoritative; all browseable post-Stage-6.9 archive
Invariant axis (PASS): - Stash byte-identity 11 cycles - Fleet pause invariance through entire 2.5+ day cycle - state.json verbatim - Append-only STATUS discipline preserved - No-amend doctrine honored - No merge to main; merge gate at Stage 5.10 with explicit LA approval per R10
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.verdict:
Stage 2 is TRULY CLOSED at all axes (structural + narrative + audit-chain + invariant). EA-6 has met "mature not minimal" comprehensively across the longest EA cycle in the procedure (~3 days; 6 disposition cycles; 55-ack chain navigation). EA-6 retires correctly; Stage 3 spawn is a separate fresh-EA cycle that EA-6 is NOT responsible for.
The LA's meta-question on handoff documents reveals an excellent procedural instinct: identifying that further handoff artifacts are needed without yet knowing whose responsibility they are. The answer (Guide-side handoff brief + Stage 3 EA init pointer are GUIDE responsibilities, not EA's) is the right delineation. Guide-#6 RECOMMENDS handoff per stage_boundary_recommendation trigger; awaits LA signal on Option A (continue) vs Option B (handoff to Guide-#7).

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
EA-6 CORRECTLY RETIRES. EA-6's responsibility for Stage 2 close ends at the closing handoff narrative (chat message); the closing handoff is COMPLETE per the closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in the EA-6 init pointer.
Stage 2 is CLOSED. The procedure transitions to Stage 3 (Copy tools), which is a SEPARATE fresh-EA spawn -- NOT EA-6's responsibility.
NEXT-TURN ACTIONS (Guide-#6 / LA):
Option A (LA selects "continue"): Guide-#6 directly authors Stage 3 EA initialization pointer in the next chat turn. The init pointer references: 947cbd5 HEAD; 12-item Stage 2 closure footnote set; the 5 EA mid-stage handoff XMLs (v1-v5) at ea_outputs/; the 6 Guide-#6 disposition files at temp_for_responses/; the 4 v4 + 4 v5 documentation corrections (D1-D5 + C1-C4); the 5 N narrative corrections; ack chain g6-ea6_n1 through n10 SATISFIED. Stage 3 EA spawns with this init pointer + 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml + STATUS.md attached.
Option B (LA selects "emit handoff"): Guide-#6 produces the brief per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml emit_format template; LA saves to GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml (overwriting prior); LA spawns Guide-#7 in fresh chat with GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + STATUS.md + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + chat-init block; Guide-#7 runs Variant B comprehension gate; Guide-#7 then authors Stage 3 EA init pointer with fresh context.
The two options differ in WHO authors the Stage 3 EA init pointer (Guide-#6 continuing vs Guide-#7 successor) but produce equivalent Stage 3 readiness. Both preserve all audit-chain content in git.
Standing invariants unchanged: no further Stage 2 commits beyond 947cbd5; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; Vikunja running per Option B until Stage 4.0/4.1 preflight.
Guide-#6 STOPS here per the stop-after-gate pattern. Awaiting LA signal on Option A vs Option B.
